title: Pulmonary Sequestration: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Pulmonary Sequestration
Identify key diagnostic features of Pulmonary Sequestration
Apply clinical reasoning to Pulmonary Sequestration management
Recognize common pitfalls in Pulmonary Sequestration

sections.title: Overview of Pulmonary Sequestration
sections.content:
**Pulmonary Sequestration**

Intralobar vs extralobar, systemic arterial supply, recurrent infections

**Key Points:**
• Non-functioning lung tissue with SYSTEMIC arterial supply (not pulmonary)
• Intralobar (75%): within visceral pleura, no own pleural covering, venous drainage to pulmonary veins
• Extralobar (25%): own pleural covering, venous drainage to systemic veins (azygos/IVC), associated with CDH

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Non-functioning lung tissue with SYSTEMIC arterial supply (not pulmonary)
• Intralobar (75%): within visceral pleura, no own pleural covering, venous drainage to pulmonary veins

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Extralobar (25%): own pleural covering, venous drainage to systemic veins (azygos/IVC), associated with CDH
• Both most common in left lower lobe
• Key diagnostic clue: anomalous systemic artery from aorta on CT angiography

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Pulmonary Sequestration:**

• Extralobar (25%): own pleural covering, venous drainage to systemic veins (azygos/IVC), associated with CDH
• Both most common in left lower lobe
• Key diagnostic clue: anomalous systemic artery from aorta on CT angiography

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Pulmonary Sequestration?

options.text: Non-functioning lung tissue with SYSTEMIC arterial supply (not pulmonary)
options.explanation: Non-functioning lung tissue with SYSTEMIC arterial supply (not pulmonary)

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Pulmonary Sequestration?

options.text: Key diagnostic clue: anomalous systemic artery from aorta on CT angiography
options.explanation: Key diagnostic clue: anomalous systemic artery from aorta on CT angiography

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with Pulmonary Sequestration. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Intralobar (75%): within visceral pleura, no own pleural covering, venous drainage to pulmonary veins
options.explanation: Intralobar (75%): within visceral pleura, no own pleural covering, venous drainage to pulmonary veins
